input.old_string:
**Decision-Needed (human judgment required):**

- [ ] [Review][Decision] **Npm tarball excludes referenced-as-contract files** — `docs/STABILITY.md` cites `test/schema/agent.js` (authoritative zod schema) and `../CHANGELOG.md` (relative link) as part of the v1.0.0 contract, but `.npmignore:7,22` excludes both from the published tarball. A downstream consumer reading `node_modules/bmad-module-skill-forge/docs/STABILITY.md` gets broken references. Options: (a) ship `test/schema/**` + `CHANGELOG.md` in the tarball via `files[]` whitelist or `.npmignore` exceptions; (b) replace relative refs with absolute GitHub URLs pinned to a commit/tag; (c) accept "contract is GitHub-only" and document that in STABILITY.md. Scope exceeds AC #8 doc-only envelope.
- [ ] [Review][Decision] **Starlight sidebar drift — `docs/STABILITY.md` not registered** — the new doc is reachable only by direct URL. `website/astro.config.mjs` Reference bucket has no entry for it. Completion Notes explicitly flagged this for a reviewer / Story 5.1 to decide. Fix is a 1-line config edit in `website/astro.config.mjs` but crosses AC #8's doc-only allowed-files envelope. Options: (a) fold into this story via a companion commit (AC #8 tweak), (b) defer to Story 5.1 pre-flight audit, (c) a standalone docs-site-nav story.
- [ ] [Review][Decision] **`test/` @internal vs `test/schema/agent.js` covered** — STABILITY.md § @internal lists "Test fixtures and test file layout under `test/` — test organization is internal" but § Skill Manifest & Frontmatter Contract simultaneously treats `test/schema/agent.js` as the authoritative source-of-truth. Resolve: (a) carve out `test/schema/**` as covered in the @internal bullet, (b) move the zod schema from `test/schema/` into a non-test location (e.g. `src/schemas/` — code change, out of docs-scope), (c) treat the inlined code block as the contract and demote `test/schema/agent.js` to "implementation detail that happens to match."
- [ ] [Review][Decision] **Node.js / OS version commitments missing from contract** — the public API contract commits to CLI subcommands, exit codes, install layout, and script aliases but is silent on supported Node.js engine range and OS (Windows vs POSIX). For a CLI shipping via npm this is a conspicuous omission — a minor Node bump could break consumers silently. Options: (a) add an `### Engine & Platform Compatibility` bullet referencing `package.json` `engines.node`, (b) explicitly declare "supported Node / OS are `@internal` at v1.0.0" (unusual but valid if intentional), (c) defer to Story 5.1 pre-flight audit with a carry-forward flag.
- [ ] [Review][Decision] **"Update preserves existing config and sidecar state" — unsupported runtime invariant** — STABILITY.md commits `skf update` to preserving config + sidecar state, but sidecar schemas are explicitly listed as `@internal`. Preserving an @internal schema requires a test-backed round-trip guarantee that doesn't exist. Options: (a) tighten to specific preserved fields (config keys in `skf-manifest.yaml`), (b) soften to "best-effort state preservation; breaking migrations will be flagged in CHANGELOG.md", (c) add a regression test (code change, out of docs-scope) and keep the commitment as-is, (d) remove the sentence.

1	---
2	title: SKF Stability & Public API Contract
3	description: What SemVer covers at v1.0.0 — the surfaces downstream consumers may pin against, and the surfaces explicitly excluded.
4	---
5	
6	> **Status:** DRAFT — locks at v1.0.0 cut (Story 5.1 pre-flight audit). Review-window open until then.
7	
8	This document is the public API contract for `bmad-module-skill-forge` at v1.0.0. It enumerates exactly which SKF surfaces [Semantic Versioning 2.0.0](https://semver.org/) covers and which are explicitly `@internal`. The goal is that a downstream consumer pinning `"bmad-module-skill-forge": "^1.0.0"` can identify — unambiguously — whether a given SKF change is breaking, additive, or internal. Breaking changes to covered surfaces require a major-version bump; additive changes to covered surfaces are minor bumps; changes to `@internal` surfaces are free to land in any release.
9	
10	## Covered Surfaces
11	
12	The five buckets below are the entirety of the v1.0.0 public surface. Anything not explicitly listed here is `@internal` (see [§ @internal — Not Covered by SemVer](#internal--not-covered-by-semver)).
13	
14	### CLI Command Surface
15	
16	The CLI is invoked via `npx bmad-module-skill-forge <subcommand>` (one-shot) or `npm run skf:<subcommand>` (post-install). Covered:
17	
18	- **Subcommands**: `install`, `update`, `status`, `uninstall`. Renaming, removing, or changing the semantics of any subcommand is breaking.
19	- **Top-level options**: `-V, --version` (prints the installed package version) and `-h, --help` (prints command help). Provided by commander as part of the entrypoint at `tools/cli/skf-cli.js`.
20	- **Per-subcommand flags**: none are currently part of the v1.0.0 surface. Each subcommand today accepts no flags. Adding a new flag is additive (minor); removing or renaming a flag once added would be breaking.
21	- **Exit codes**: `0` on success (including user-cancel on `install` and "nothing to do" paths on `uninstall`); `1` on unhandled failure. Intermediate exit codes are not part of the contract.
22	- **`npm run skf:*` script aliases**: `skf:install`, `skf:update`, `skf:status`, `skf:uninstall` in `package.json` are the post-install convenience entry points. Renaming or removing any `skf:*` script is breaking. (Note: `release:*` scripts are NOT part of this contract — they are internal-to-maintainers and are being retired across Epic 4 and Epic 6.)
23	
24	### Programmatic API of `tools/cli/skf-cli.js`
25	
26	`tools/cli/skf-cli.js` is the CLI entrypoint for `npx bmad-module-skill-forge` and for `npm run skf:<subcommand>`. Though `package.json`'s `main` field points at this file (standard npm resolution for bin-like packages), it has **no programmatic export surface**. The file runs commander setup and calls `program.parse(process.argv)` as top-level side effects; it has no `module.exports` block. A `require('bmad-module-skill-forge')` from Node runs the CLI parser against the caller's `process.argv` — not a supported usage.
27	
28	Programmatic embedding of SKF is explicitly `@internal` at v1.0.0. If a programmatic API is ever added, it will be introduced via an explicit `module.exports` block and a minor version bump (additive).
29	
30	### Skill Manifest & Frontmatter Contract
31	
32	**`SKILL.md` frontmatter.** SKF-shipped `SKILL.md` files conform to the [agentskills.io minimal frontmatter spec](https://github.com/agentskills/agentskills). Required fields are `name` (kebab-case) and `description` (one-line natural-language summary that terminates with an explicit trigger phrase — see the `src/skf-*/SKILL.md` files in this repo for live examples). SKF does NOT extend the agentskills.io spec; there are no SKF-specific frontmatter fields. If the agentskills.io spec evolves, SKF tracks upstream rather than forking.
33	
34	**`bmad-skill-manifest.yaml` schema.** The agent-type skill manifest shape SKF uses is validated by the zod schema at `test/schema/agent.js`:
35	
36	```js
37	const manifestSchema = z
38	  .object({
39	    type: z.enum(['agent', 'workflow', 'tool']),   // required
40	    name: createNonEmptyString('name'),            // required
41	    displayName: createNonEmptyString('displayName').optional(),
42	    title: createNonEmptyString('title').optional(),
43	    icon: z.string().optional(),
44	    capabilities: z.string().optional(),
45	    role: createNonEmptyString('role').optional(),
46	    identity: createNonEmptyString('identity').optional(),
47	    communicationStyle: createNonEmptyString('communicationStyle').optional(),
48	    principles: createNonEmptyString('principles').optional(),
49	    module: createNonEmptyString('module'),        // required
50	  })
51	  .strict();
52	```
53	
54	The schema is strict — unknown keys are rejected. Renaming or removing a required field, changing an enum value, or tightening an optional field into required is breaking. Adding a new optional field is additive. The `module` field is always `skf` for SKF-authored manifests.
55	
56	### Installation Layout
57	
58	When a consumer runs `skf install` in a project, SKF creates or modifies exactly the following on-disk paths. Paths outside this list are not touched (no surprise edits to project root). Renaming any path, changing a path's semantics, or removing a path from this list is breaking.
59	
60	- `_bmad/skf/` — SKF module files.
61	- `_bmad/_config/skf-manifest.yaml` — install manifest. Format per `tools/cli/lib/manifest.js`; covered top-level keys: `version`, `installed_at`, `action`, `module`, `skf_folder`, `ides`, `skills_output_folder`, `forge_data_folder`, `directories[]`, `files.{skf,sidecar,ide_skills,learning,output}`. Downstream tooling that introspects an SKF install (e.g. to detect installed IDEs or list installed skills) reads this file; its top-level shape is the v1.0.0 contract.
62	- `_bmad/_memory/forger-sidecar/` — Ferris agent runtime state directory. The directory's existence and location are covered; the internal file structure and schemas inside are `@internal` (stable as a runtime contract between SKF versions during the life of one install, but not a downstream-consumable API).
63	- `_skf-learn/` — optional learning material; created only when the user opts into learning at install time.
64	- `{skills_output_folder}/` — default `skills/`, configurable via install prompt and persisted to `skf-manifest.yaml`.
65	- `{forge_data_folder}/` — default `forge-data/`, configurable via install prompt and persisted to `skf-manifest.yaml`.
66	- **Per-IDE skill directories.** SKF installs skills to each configured IDE's conventional skills directory. The IDE → directory mapping is enumerated in `tools/cli/lib/platform-codes.yaml` under `platforms.<ide>.installer.target_dir`; that file is the source of truth and is itself part of the v1.0.0 contract. Specifically: renaming `platforms.*` or `installer.target_dir` is breaking; removing a platform's `target_dir` is breaking for consumers of that IDE; adding a new platform entry is additive (minor).
67	
68	### User-Facing Command Output Formats
69	
70	The v1.0.0 contract commits to the **information each command outputs** and to **exit-code semantics**, not to exact human-readable wording, ANSI color choices, or console layout. Downstream tooling that needs structured introspection of an install should read `_bmad/_config/skf-manifest.yaml` directly (see [§ Installation Layout](#installation-layout)), which IS committed verbatim.
71	
72	- **`skf status`** SHALL convey: installation-present boolean, installed version string, forge-tier name (or a "not detected" equivalent), configured IDE list, workflow-skill count, sidecar state, and output-folder paths. Exit `0` on success; `1` on unhandled failure.
73	- **`skf install`** SHALL exit `0` on successful install and on user-cancel, `1` on unhandled failure. The success path SHALL emit a message referencing the SKF folder path created in the project.
74	- **`skf update`** SHALL exit `0` on successful update; `1` on unhandled failure. Update preserves existing config and sidecar state.
75	- **`skf uninstall`** SHALL exit `0` on successful removal, on "nothing to do" (no prior install detected), and on user-declined confirmation. Exit `1` on unhandled failure.
76	
77	Explicit non-commitments: ANSI color choices, exact prose wording, precise layout of tables or lists, and i18n prose variations are `@internal` and may change in minor releases.
78	
79	## @internal — Not Covered by SemVer
80	
81	The following surfaces are explicitly excluded from the v1.0.0 contract. Changes to any of these may land in any release — patch, minor, or major — without constituting a "breaking change" from a SemVer perspective. Downstream consumers SHOULD NOT pin against these surfaces.
82	
83	- **`tools/cli/lib/*` internal helper modules** — implementation detail of the CLI; not a library. Any refactor that preserves the observable CLI surface and install layout above is allowed.
84	- **Internal structure of workflow step files under `src/skf-*/steps-c/`** — the workflow authoring format is an SKF-internal authoring surface; step numbering, file names, and prose can change.
85	- **`_bmad/_memory/forger-sidecar/*.yaml` file schemas** — Ferris sidecar state. Stable as a runtime contract between SKF versions during one install's lifetime (so `skf update` works), but not a downstream-consumable schema.
86	- **Ferris agent persona prose and menu wording** — the in-product agent persona can be rephrased or restructured at any time.
87	- **Exact chalk styling / ANSI color choices in command output** — stylistic; see the output-format bucket above for what IS committed.
88	- **Internal logic of the `Installer` class in `tools/cli/lib/installer.js`** — private implementation; what's covered is the observable install layout, not how the files get there.
89	- **Workflow `{communication_language}` / `{document_output_language}` template substitution** — an authoring convenience for workflow prose; the mechanism is internal.
90	- **Knowledge-base contents under `src/knowledge/` and `src/skf-*/references/`** — evolving reference material, not a schema.
91	- **Test fixtures and test file layout under `test/`** — test organization is internal.
92	- **Build and docs tooling (`tools/build-docs.js`, `website/`)** — internal build pipeline for the Starlight docs site; not a consumer surface.
93	- **`_bmad-output/` planning-artifact layout** — gitignored and never distributed; not shipped in the npm package.
94	
95	## How to Use This Contract
96	
97	Pin SKF as a dependency with caret-range major-version pinning to get minor and patch updates automatically:
98	
99	```json
100	"bmad-module-skill-forge": "^1.0.0"
101	```
102	
103	The caret range covers additive changes and internal refactors on covered surfaces, and any change on `@internal` surfaces. If you find that an SKF release broke a covered surface listed in this document, please open an issue at the SKF issue tracker citing the relevant section — it is either a bug in SKF that needs reverting or a missed major-version bump.
104	
105	If a surface you depend on is NOT explicitly enumerated in [§ Covered Surfaces](#covered-surfaces), treat it as `@internal` — if in doubt, it is `@internal`. If you believe a surface should be promoted from `@internal` to covered, open an issue with a use-case description; promotion is additive and can land in a minor release.
106	
107	## Changes to This Contract
108	
109	Shrinking the covered surface (removing a commitment) is a breaking change and requires a major-version bump. Expanding the covered surface (promoting an `@internal` surface to covered, or adding a new commitment) is additive and ships in a minor release. This document is versioned alongside the package; historical versions are visible via `git log docs/STABILITY.md`.
110	
111	## References
112	
113	- [agentskills.io spec](https://github.com/agentskills/agentskills) — external canonical source for the `SKILL.md` frontmatter grammar.
114	- [Semantic Versioning 2.0.0](https://semver.org/) — the SemVer semantics this contract commits to.
115	- [CHANGELOG.md](../CHANGELOG.md) — release-by-release history; breaking changes are called out there.
116	- [docs/RELEASING.md](RELEASING.md) — maintainer reference for the release pipeline (companion document: this file is about what ships, `RELEASING.md` is about how).
117	- `tools/cli/lib/platform-codes.yaml` — source of truth for the IDE → skills-directory mapping covered under [§ Installation Layout](#installation-layout).
118	- `test/schema/agent.js` — zod schema definitions for `bmad-skill-manifest.yaml` covered under [§ Skill Manifest & Frontmatter Contract](#skill-manifest--frontmatter-contract).
119

- **Per-subcommand flags**: none are currently part of the v1.0.0 surface. Each subcommand today accepts no flags. Adding a new flag is additive (minor); removing or renaming a flag once added would be breaking.

- **`npm run skf:*` script aliases**: `skf:install`, `skf:update`, `skf:status`, `skf:uninstall` in `package.json` are the post-install convenience entry points. Renaming or removing any `skf:*` script is breaking. (Note: `release:*` scripts are NOT part of this contract — they are internal-to-maintainers and are being retired across Epic 4 and Epic 6.)

**`SKILL.md` frontmatter.** SKF-shipped `SKILL.md` files conform to the [agentskills.io minimal frontmatter spec](https://github.com/agentskills/agentskills). ```js
const manifestSchema = z
  .object({
    type: z.enum(['agent', 'workflow', 'tool']),   // required
    name: createNonEmptyString('name'),            // required
    displayName: createNonEmptyString('displayName').optional(),
    title: createNonEmptyString('title').optional(),
    icon: z.string().optional(),
    capabilities: z.string().optional(),
    role: createNonEmptyString('role').optional(),
    identity: createNonEmptyString('identity').optional(),

- `_bmad/_config/skf-manifest.yaml` — install manifest. - `_bmad/_memory/forger-sidecar/` — Ferris agent runtime state directory. The directory's existence and location are covered; the internal file structure and schemas inside are `@internal` (stable as a runtime contract between SKF versions during the life of one install, but not a downstream-consumable API).
- `_skf-learn/` — optional learning material; created only when the user opts into learning at install time.
- `{skills_output_folder}/` — default `skills/`, configurable via install prompt and persisted to `skf-manifest.yaml`.
- `{forge_data_folder}/` — default `forge-data/`, configurable via install prompt and persisted to `skf-manifest.yaml`.

- **`tools/cli/lib/*` internal helper modules** — implementation detail of the CLI; not a library. Any refactor that preserves the observable CLI surface and install layout above is allowed.
- **Internal structure of workflow step files under `src/skf-*/steps-c/`** — the workflow authoring format is an SKF-internal authoring surface; step numbering, file names, and prose can change.
- **`_bmad/_memory/forger-sidecar/*.yaml` file schemas** — Ferris sidecar state. Stable as a runtime contract between SKF versions during one install's lifetime (so `skf update` works), but not a downstream-consumable schema.
- **Ferris agent persona prose and menu wording** — the in-product agent persona can be rephrased or restructured at any time.

```json

60	 * @param {string} filePath Path to the manifest file.

62	 * @returns {import('zod').SafeParseReturnType<unknown, unknown>} SafeParse result.
63	 */
64	function validateManifestFile(filePath, manifestYaml) {
65	  return manifestSchema.safeParse(manifestYaml);
66	}

68	const manifestSchema = z
69	  .object({
70	    type: z.enum(['agent', 'workflow', 'tool'], {
71	      errorMap: () => ({ message: 'type must be one of: agent, workflow, tool' }),
72	    }),
73	    name: createNonEmptyString('name'),
74	    displayName: createNonEmptyString('displayName').optional(),
75	    title: createNonEmptyString('title').optional(),
76	    icon: z.string().optional(),
77	    capabilities: z.string().optional(),
78	    role: createNonEmptyString('role').optional(),
79	    identity: createNonEmptyString('identity').optional(),
80	    communicationStyle: createNonEmptyString('communicationStyle').optional(),
81	    principles: createNonEmptyString('principles').optional(),
82	    module: createNonEmptyString('module'),
83	  })
84	  .strict();
85	
86	module.exports = { validateAgentFile, validateManifestFile };

input.old_string:
- **Subcommands**: `install`, `update`, `status`, `uninstall`. Renaming, removing, or changing the semantics of any subcommand is breaking.
- **Top-level options**: `-V, --version` (prints the installed package version) and `-h, --help` (prints command help). Provided by commander as part of the entrypoint at `tools/cli/skf-cli.js`.
- **Per-subcommand flags**: none are currently part of the v1.0.0 surface. Each subcommand today accepts no flags. Adding a new flag is additive (minor); removing or renaming a flag once added would be breaking.
- **Exit codes**: `0` on success (including user-cancel on `install` and "nothing to do" paths on `uninstall`); `1` on unhandled failure. Intermediate exit codes are not part of the contract.
- **`npm run skf:*` script aliases**: `skf:install`, `skf:update`, `skf:status`, `skf:uninstall` in `package.json` are the post-install convenience entry points. Renaming or removing any `skf:*` script is breaking. (Note: `release:*` scripts are NOT part of this contract — they are internal-to-maintainers and are being retired across Epic 4 and Epic 6.)

Only `0` and `1` are reserved today; introducing additional exit-code values in the future is additive (minor) and will be called out in `CHANGELOG.md`.

### Programmatic API of `tools/cli/skf-cli.js`

input.old_string: `tools/cli/skf-cli.js` is the CLI entrypoint for `npx bmad-module-skill-forge` and for `npm run skf:<subcommand>`. Though `package.json`'s `main` field points at this file (standard npm resolution for bin-like packages), it has **no programmatic export surface**. The file runs commander setup and calls `program.parse(process.argv)` as top-level side effects; it has no `module.exports` block. A `require('bmad-module-skill-forge')` from Node runs the CLI parser against the caller's `process.argv` — not a supported usage.
input.new_string: `tools/cli/skf-cli.js` is the CLI logic for `npx bmad-module-skill-forge` (invoked via a thin `bin` wrapper at `tools/skf-npx-wrapper.js` that execs `skf-cli.js`) and for `npm run skf:<subcommand>`. Though `package.json`'s `main` field points at `skf-cli.js`, it has **no programmatic export surface**. The file runs commander setup and calls `program.parse(process.argv)` as top-level side effects; it has no `module.exports` block. A `require('bmad-module-skill-forge')` from Node runs the CLI parser against the caller's `process.argv` — not a supported usage.

lines: **`SKILL.md` frontmatter.** SKF-shipped `SKILL.md` files conform to the [agentskills.io minimal frontmatter spec](https://github.com/agentskills/agentskills). Required fields are `name` (kebab-case) and `description` (one-line natural-language summary that terminates with an explicit trigger phrase — see the `src/skf-*/SKILL.md` files in this repo for live examples). SKF does NOT extend the agentskills.io spec; there are no SKF-specific frontmatter fields. If the agentskills.io spec evolves, SKF tracks upstream rather than forking.

### Installation Layout

input.old_string:
When a consumer runs `skf install` in a project, SKF creates or modifies exactly the following on-disk paths. Paths outside this list are not touched (no surprise edits to project root). Renaming any path, changing a path's semantics, or removing a path from this list is breaking.

- `_bmad/skf/` — SKF module files.
- `_bmad/_config/skf-manifest.yaml` — install manifest. Format per `tools/cli/lib/manifest.js`; covered top-level keys: `version`, `installed_at`, `action`, `module`, `skf_folder`, `ides`, `skills_output_folder`, `forge_data_folder`, `directories[]`, `files.{skf,sidecar,ide_skills,learning,output}`. Downstream tooling that introspects an SKF install (e.g. to detect installed IDEs or list installed skills) reads this file; its top-level shape is the v1.0.0 contract.
- `_bmad/_memory/forger-sidecar/` — Ferris agent runtime state directory. The directory's existence and location are covered; the internal file structure and schemas inside are `@internal` (stable as a runtime contract between SKF versions during the life of one install, but not a downstream-consumable API).
- `_skf-learn/` — optional learning material; created only when the user opts into learning at install time.
- `{skills_output_folder}/` — default `skills/`, configurable via install prompt and persisted to `skf-manifest.yaml`.
- `{forge_data_folder}/` — default `forge-data/`, configurable via install prompt and persisted to `skf-manifest.yaml`.
- **Per-IDE skill directories.** SKF installs skills to each configured IDE's conventional skills directory. The IDE → directory mapping is enumerated in `tools/cli/lib/platform-codes.yaml` under `platforms.<ide>.installer.target_dir`; that file is the source of truth and is itself part of the v1.0.0 contract. Specifically: renaming `platforms.*` or `installer.target_dir` is breaking; removing a platform's `target_dir` is breaking for consumers of that IDE; adding a new platform entry is additive (minor).
input.new_string:
When a consumer runs `skf install` in a project, SKF creates or modifies the following on-disk paths (some names are configurable at install time; defaults are shown). Renaming any path, changing a path's semantics, or removing a path from this list is breaking.

- `_bmad/skf/` — SKF module files.
- `_bmad/_config/skf-manifest.yaml` — install manifest. Format per `tools/cli/lib/manifest.js`; covered top-level keys: `version` (equals the SKF package version at install time — downstream tooling may read it to detect the installed SKF version), `installed_at`, `action`, `module`, `skf_folder`, `ides`, `skills_output_folder`, `forge_data_folder`, `directories[]`, `files.{skf,sidecar,ide_skills,learning,output}`. Downstream tooling that introspects an SKF install (e.g. to detect installed IDEs or list installed skills) reads this file; its top-level shape is the v1.0.0 contract.
- `_bmad/_memory/forger-sidecar/` — Ferris agent runtime state directory. The directory's existence and location are covered. File formats inside are `@internal` (not a downstream-consumable schema); `skf update` round-trips sidecar state between SKF versions — see `skf update` in [§ User-Facing Command Output Formats](#user-facing-command-output-formats) for the state-preservation commitment.
- `_skf-learn/` — optional learning material; created only when the user opts into learning at install time.
- `{skills_output_folder}/` — default `skills/`, configurable via install prompt and persisted to `skf-manifest.yaml`.
- `{forge_data_folder}/` — default `forge-data/`, configurable via install prompt and persisted to `skf-manifest.yaml`.
- `.gitignore` at project root — SKF appends `_bmad/_memory/` as an ignore entry (or creates the file if absent). Existing content is preserved; if the entry is already present, the file is left untouched. The appended entry name is part of the contract.
- **Per-IDE skill directories.** SKF installs skills to each configured IDE's conventional skills directory. The IDE → directory mapping is enumerated in `tools/cli/lib/platform-codes.yaml` under `platforms.<ide>.installer.target_dir`; the `platforms.<ide>.installer.target_dir` mapping is part of the v1.0.0 contract (other keys in that file are `@internal`). Renaming `platforms.*` or `installer.target_dir` is breaking; removing a platform's `target_dir` is breaking for consumers of that IDE; adding a new platform entry is additive (minor).

### User-Facing Command Output Formats

input.old_string:
- **`skf status`** SHALL convey: installation-present boolean, installed version string, forge-tier name (or a "not detected" equivalent), configured IDE list, workflow-skill count, sidecar state, and output-folder paths. Exit `0` on success; `1` on unhandled failure.
- **`skf install`** SHALL exit `0` on successful install and on user-cancel, `1` on unhandled failure. The success path SHALL emit a message referencing the SKF folder path created in the project.
- **`skf update`** SHALL exit `0` on successful update; `1` on unhandled failure. Update preserves existing config and sidecar state.
- **`skf uninstall`** SHALL exit `0` on successful removal, on "nothing to do" (no prior install detected), and on user-declined confirmation. Exit `1` on unhandled failure.
input.new_string:
- **`skf status`** SHALL convey: installation-present boolean, installed version string, forge-tier name (or a "not detected" equivalent), configured IDE list, workflow-skill count, sidecar state, and output-folder paths. Exit `0` on success; `1` on unhandled failure.
- **`skf install`** SHALL exit `0` on successful install and on user-cancel (Ctrl-C at a prompt or explicit decline at a confirm step); `1` on unhandled failure. The success path SHALL emit a success confirmation.
- **`skf update`** SHALL exit `0` on successful update and on "nothing to do" (no prior install detected); `1` on unhandled failure. `skf update` aims to preserve existing config and sidecar state across SKF versions; breaking migrations, when needed, will be called out in `CHANGELOG.md`.
- **`skf uninstall`** SHALL exit `0` on successful removal, on "nothing to do" (no prior install detected), and on user-declined confirmation. Exit `1` on unhandled failure.

### Engine & Platform Compatibility

SKF supports the Node.js major versions declared in `package.json` `engines.node` (currently `>=22.0.0`). Dropping a supported major is breaking; adding support for a new major is additive. Linux, macOS, and Windows are supported runtime platforms; dropping OS support is breaking. Adding support for a new platform is additive.

input.old_string:
- [agentskills.io spec](https://github.com/agentskills/agentskills) — external canonical source for the `SKILL.md` frontmatter grammar.
- [Semantic Versioning 2.0.0](https://semver.org/) — the SemVer semantics this contract commits to.
- [CHANGELOG.md](../CHANGELOG.md) — release-by-release history; breaking changes are called out there.

lines: **Patch (unambiguous fixes within docs-only scope) — 14 original + 4 from resolved decisions = 18 total:**
-- [ ] [Review][Patch] **D1 resolution** — Replace `../CHANGELOG.md` relative link and `test/schema/agent.js` bare path reference with absolute GitHub URLs [docs/STABILITY.md § References].

-- [ ] [Review][Patch] **D3 resolution** — Carve out `test/schema/**` from the `test/` @internal bullet: "Test fixtures and test file layout under `test/` — test organization is internal (**except `test/schema/**`, which defines schemas covered by this contract**)" [docs/STABILITY.md § @internal].

-- [ ] [Review][Patch] **D4 resolution** — Add new subsection `### Engine & Platform Compatibility` under § Covered Surfaces: commit supported Node.js versions track `package.json engines.node` (dropping a major is breaking; adding one is additive) and OS support for Linux/macOS/Windows (dropping OS support is breaking) [docs/STABILITY.md § Covered Surfaces].

-- [ ] [Review][Patch] **D5 resolution** — Soften "Update preserves existing config and sidecar state." to "`skf update` aims to preserve existing config and sidecar state across versions; breaking migrations, when needed, will be called out in `CHANGELOG.md`." [docs/STABILITY.md § User-Facing Command Output Formats — `skf update` bullet].

-- [ ] [Review][Patch] `.gitignore` modification missing from Installation Layout [docs/STABILITY.md § Installation Layout] — `tools/cli/lib/installer.js` appends to `.gitignore` at install time, but STABILITY.md says "SKF creates or modifies exactly the following on-disk paths. Paths outside this list are not touched (no surprise edits to project root)" and omits `.gitignore`. Either add `.gitignore` as a bullet (with the specific appended entries) or soften "exactly" and "no surprise edits" wording.

-- [ ] [Review][Patch] `skf install` success-message claim contradicts code [docs/STABILITY.md § User-Facing Command Output Formats] — STABILITY.md says the install success path "SHALL emit a message referencing the SKF folder path", but `tools/cli/lib/ui.js` `displaySuccess` receives `skfFolder` as a parameter and does not emit it. Options: (a) soften to "SHALL emit a success confirmation" (docs-only), (b) code-change to echo the path (out of docs-scope).

-- [ ] [Review][Patch] `npx bmad-module-skill-forge` wrapper indirection mislabeled [docs/STABILITY.md § Programmatic API] — claim that `npx` invokes `tools/cli/skf-cli.js` directly; `package.json` `bin` actually points at `tools/skf-npx-wrapper.js` which execs `skf-cli.js`. One-line clarification: note the wrapper + exec indirection.

-- [ ] [Review][Patch] `skf update` "no prior install" exit-0 undocumented [docs/STABILITY.md § User-Facing Command Output Formats] — `tools/cli/commands/update.js` exits 0 when no prior install is detected; contract only covers success + unhandled-failure. Add a "nothing to do (no prior install detected)" clause mirroring `skf uninstall`.

-- [ ] [Review][Patch] Inlined zod snippet drops `errorMap` option [docs/STABILITY.md § Skill Manifest & Frontmatter Contract] — transcription from `test/schema/agent.js:68-84` omits the `errorMap` on `z.enum`. Either include it verbatim or label the block "simplified — see `test/schema/agent.js` for exact source."

-- [ ] [Review][Patch] Top-level flag vs per-subcommand-flag claim reads as contradictory [docs/STABILITY.md § CLI Command Surface] — "`-V, --version`" and "`-h, --help`" are committed at top level, then "Per-subcommand flags: none". Commander provides `-h/--help` on every subcommand by default. Clarify: note that `-h/--help` is also available per-subcommand (commander-default) and is covered, or reword to avoid the apparent contradiction.

-- [ ] [Review][Patch] "Intermediate exit codes are not part of the contract" is confusing [docs/STABILITY.md § CLI Command Surface] — reads as if mid-execution exit codes somehow differ. Rewrite: "Only exit codes `0` (success) and `1` (unhandled failure) are reserved; future exit-code values may be added additively in minor releases."

-- [ ] [Review][Patch] Forge-sidecar: "covered + @internal + stable across updates" is confusing [docs/STABILITY.md § Installation Layout and § @internal] — the directory is covered (existence + location), its internals are @internal, but "stable as a runtime contract between SKF versions during one install's lifetime (so `skf update` works)." Restate: `skf update` round-trips sidecar state between versions; the file formats inside are @internal (not directly consumable by downstream tools).

-- [ ] [Review][Patch] Install layout "exactly the following paths" contradicts configurable path names [docs/STABILITY.md § Installation Layout] — the list commits `{skills_output_folder}/` (default `skills/`) and `{forge_data_folder}/` (default `forge-data/`) as user-configurable. Reword "exactly the following paths" → "the following paths (some names configurable at install time; defaults shown)".

-- [ ] [Review][Patch] `platform-codes.yaml` "whole file is the contract" over-commits [docs/STABILITY.md § Installation Layout — per-IDE skill directories] — text asserts the YAML "is itself part of the v1.0.0 contract" then carves out two specific key paths. Other keys in the file are not part of the contract. Replace "the file is itself part of the v1.0.0 contract" with "the `platforms.<ide>.installer.target_dir` mapping in that file is part of the contract; other keys are `@internal`."

-- [ ] [Review][Patch] `module` field ambiguity — consumer-supplied vs SKF-injected [docs/STABILITY.md § Skill Manifest & Frontmatter Contract] — schema lists `module` as a required field, prose says "the `module` field is always `skf` for SKF-authored manifests." Unclear whether consumers writing manifests must supply `module: skf` verbatim or whether SKF injects it. Clarify consumer-authoring expectation.

-- [ ] [Review][Patch] Missing issue-tracker URL [docs/STABILITY.md § How to Use This Contract] — "open an issue at the SKF issue tracker" is a dead-end pointer for a downstream consumer. Add the GitHub Issues URL.

-- [ ] [Review][Patch] "User-cancel" for `skf install` not defined [docs/STABILITY.md § CLI Command Surface — Exit codes + § User-Facing Command Output Formats] — contract commits `install` to exit 0 on user-cancel without defining the cancel surface. Add a parenthetical naming the concrete cancel paths (Ctrl-C at a prompt, or declining a confirm step).

-- [ ] [Review][Patch] `manifest.version` semantics unspecified [docs/STABILITY.md § Installation Layout] — manifest contract lists `version` as a covered top-level key but doesn't say what it means. Add: "`version` equals the SKF package version at install time — downstream tools may read it to detect the installed SKF version."

input.command:
git add docs/STABILITY.md && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
docs(stability): address code-review findings (18 patches)

Parallel adversarial code review of `2fc1069` (Blind Hunter + Edge Case
Hunter + Acceptance Auditor) surfaced 18 patches plus 5 decision-needed
items (all resolved in-story) and 6 defer entries (logged to
`_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/deferred-work.md`). Acceptance
Auditor returned no AC violations; findings were contract-precision and
documentation-fidelity issues.

Substantive changes:

- New `### Engine & Platform Compatibility` subsection commits Node.js
  support tracking `package.json` `engines.node` (currently `>=22.0.0`)
  and Linux/macOS/Windows OS support, closing a gap in the v1.0.0
  surface (D4).
- Added `.gitignore` to the install-layout list — `tools/cli/lib/installer.js`
  appends `_bmad/_memory/` at install time, previously uncovered (P1).
- Absolute GitHub URLs for `CHANGELOG.md`, `platform-codes.yaml`, and
  `test/schema/agent.js` in § References so links resolve for consumers
  reading from the npm tarball (where `.npmignore` excludes those paths);
  `docs/RELEASING.md` stays relative as a same-folder sibling (D1).
- Carved out `test/schema/**` from the `test/` @internal bullet — the
  zod schema is authoritative for the manifest contract, so the @internal
  wholesale exclusion needed narrowing (D3).
- Softened `skf update` state-preservation commitment from invariant to
  best-effort with CHANGELOG.md escalation for breaking migrations —
  sidecar file formats remain @internal, so the prior wording over-
  promised (D5).
- Clarified npx wrapper indirection (`tools/skf-npx-wrapper.js` execs
  `skf-cli.js`); user-cancel surface for `skf install` (Ctrl-C or
  decline); `skf update` "nothing to do" exit-0 path; `manifest.version`
  field semantics; `-h/--help` availability per-subcommand;
  `platforms.<ide>.installer.target_dir` carve-out in
  `platform-codes.yaml`; `module` field consumer-authoring expectation
  vs SKF-injected; zod snippet labeled "simplified" with pointer to
  live source; GitHub Issues URL for the contract-breakage report path.
- Reworded Installation Layout intro ("exactly these paths" → "the
  following paths, some names configurable at install time") and the
  forge-sidecar bullet to stop conflating covered (directory) vs
  @internal (file formats) vs runtime-preserved (update round-trip).
- Replaced the "intermediate exit codes not part of the contract" line
  with an additive-on-minor commitment for future codes beyond 0/1.

Quality gates re-run green:

- `npm test` — all 12 subcommands (test:schemas, test:install, test:cli,
  test:workflow, test:python, test:knowledge, validate:schemas,
  validate:skills, validate:refs, lint, lint:md, format:check)
- `npm run docs:validate-links` — 16 files, 0 issues
- `validate:refs` — 269 references, 180 files, 0 broken

Scope: docs/STABILITY.md only (+23 / -18, net +5 lines). No
package.json, workflow, source, or test changes. DRAFT banner
preserved — Story 5.1 pre-flight audit still owns the final lock.

Deferred (6 items in `deferred-work.md`): Starlight sidebar entry for
STABILITY.md (D2, Story 5.1 handoff); `commands/install.js` silent-
exit-0 on `{success: false}` (code fix); `package.json main` pointing
at side-effectful CLI (pre-v1.0.0 housekeeping); RFC 2119 keyword
conventions (prose hygiene); SKILL.md `description` trigger-phrase
grammar (upstream agentskills.io territory); caret-range guidance
pre-v1.0.0 (Story 5.1 lock flip).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -1
